Hi Rafael, this is Diana. The board was happy with your presentation and your Q and A last week. Now there is one final skill for a great meeting: closing it well. A meeting is only useful if everyone leaves knowing what to do next. So here is your task. At the end of the meeting, do three things. First, summarize the main points in one or two sentences, so nobody is confused. Second, agree on the action items, the clear tasks for after the meeting. Third, assign an owner to each task, so everyone knows who is responsible for what. For example, you can say, To sum up, we agreed on the new budget. The next step is to send the proposal. Carla will be responsible for the report, and I will take care of the contract. Always end with a short recap and a clear deadline. A good outcome is when the minutes show who does what, and by when. See you at the meeting, and good luck closing it.

Thank you all for a productive meeting. Before we finish, let me wrap up. To sum up, we agreed on three things today. First, we will move forward with the new supplier. Second, we approved the budget for the next quarter. Third, the team will start the project in March. Now let us assign the action items. I will be responsible for sending the summary by email tonight. Carla is responsible for preparing the minutes, and Bruno will take care of the schedule. The owner of each task is clear, so nothing is forgotten. The main outcome of this meeting is a simple plan that everyone understands. I will send a short recap with the deadlines this afternoon. Let us conclude here. Thank you for your time, and have a great day.

Diana asks Rafael to close the meeting well. First, Rafael summarizes the main points so nobody is confused. Then he agrees on the action items, the clear tasks for after the meeting. Next, he assigns an owner to each task, so everyone knows who is responsible. Finally, he sends a short recap with the deadlines, and the minutes show who does what.
